steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures around the blank
steps.content: On the left: a relative clause with a prepositional phrase embedded parenthetically within it. On the right: a passive infinitive that lacks a modal verb.

steps.title: Apply the parenthetical interruption rule
steps.content:
When a parenthetical phrase interrupts a clause, commas must set it off on both sides.

The blank requires “malleability,” to close the parenthetical phrase, plus “could” to provide the required modal verb for “be easily carved.” The other choices fail to include the closing comma, use unconventional punctuation instead, or break the verb structure.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
